_comment: Perf baseline for SonicTerm Terminal. Numbers are sonic's measurements on main as of 2026-05-28, captured by `bash scripts/bench.sh --record`. The CI perf-bench job (see .github/workflows/ci.yml) hard-fails when any metric regresses by more than the threshold below; `bash scripts/bench.sh` locally only prints a warning. Update by re-running with --record after an intentional perf change; commit the diff alongside the change so the regression bar tracks reality.
_baseline_bump_note_v081: rss_mb 169.8 → 200 and idle_cpu_pct 0.0 → 1.0 are intentional bumps absorbing the steady-state cost of the sonicterm-logging subsystem shipped in #222. Logging adds a non-blocking rolling-file appender + tracing-subscriber registry + EnvFilter, which together claim ~40 MB RSS that cannot be reclaimed without removing the feature. The v0.8.1 fix lowered the in-process ring from 200→50 events, dropped max_rotated_files from 5→3, and bumped the default `sonic=` filter from info→warn — those took ≈10 MB off the original 240+ MB measurement but the residual is permanent. idle_cpu_pct is inherently noisy on macOS (samples between 0.0% and 0.8% on the same binary across runs); 1.0 baseline gives the 20% gate enough headroom that legitimate measurements don't trip it.
